questions.name: 1.1_aaa_DP900PA
questions.text: Which type of data structure should you use to optimize create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations for data saved in a multi-column tabular format?
questions.textHtml: Which type of data structure should you use to optimize create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations for data saved in a multi-column tabular format?
questions.rationale:
A relational database is the best option for CRUD operations and uses the least amount of storage space. A key/value store is used for simple lookups based on a single key to obtain a single value. A document database uses unstructured data such as JSON, and is optimized for retrieval, not CRUD operations. A graph database is used to store hierarchical data, such as organizational charts that have nodes and edges.

[Explore databases - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-core-data-concepts/4-databases?ns-enrollment-type=learningpath&ns-enrollment-id=learn.wwl.azure-data-fundamentals-explore-core-data-concepts)

[Understand data store models - Azure Application Architecture Guide | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/guide/technology-choices/data-store-overview)
questions.rationaleHtml:
A relational database is the best option for CRUD operations and uses the least amount of storage space. A key/value store is used for simple lookups based on a single key to obtain a single value. A document database uses unstructured data such as JSON, and is optimized for retrieval, not CRUD operations. A graph database is used to store hierarchical data, such as organizational charts that have nodes and edges.

Explore databases - Training | Microsoft Learn

Understand data store models - Azure Application Architecture Guide | Microsoft Learn

questions.name: 1.2_aba_DP900PA
questions.text:
You need to aggregate and store multiple JSON files that contain records for sales transactions. The solution must minimize the development effort.

Which storage solution should you implement?
questions.textHtml:
You need to aggregate and store multiple JSON files that contain records for sales transactions. The solution must minimize the development effort.

Which storage solution should you implement?
questions.rationale:
Azure Cosmos DB has a SQL API that is optimized to store and process (transform) JSON files. The SQL API allows you to query the documents by using SQL-like language. There is no additional learning curve here to complete the task. Azure Files is used as storage for any type of file. There are no built-in methods to query the file and aggregate data. Blob storage allows you to store any type of data. You must use Azure Synapse Analytics or Azure Databricks to be able to query and aggregate the data. Azure SQL Database is a relational database that keeps data in tables. You must create a process that queries the JSON files and stores them in a relational format.

[Explore file storage - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-core-data-concepts/3-file-storage)

[Explore databases - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-core-data-concepts/4-databases)
questions.rationaleHtml:
Azure Cosmos DB has a SQL API that is optimized to store and process (transform) JSON files. The SQL API allows you to query the documents by using SQL-like language. There is no additional learning curve here to complete the task. Azure Files is used as storage for any type of file. There are no built-in methods to query the file and aggregate data. Blob storage allows you to store any type of data. You must use Azure Synapse Analytics or Azure Databricks to be able to query and aggregate the data. Azure SQL Database is a relational database that keeps data in tables. You must create a process that queries the JSON files and stores them in a relational format.

Explore file storage - Training | Microsoft Learn

Explore databases - Training | Microsoft Learn

questions.name: 1.2_abb_DP900PA
questions.text: Which is the best type of database to use for an organizational chart?
questions.textHtml: Which is the best type of database to use for an organizational chart?
questions.rationale:
Graph databases are the best option for hierarchical data. Azure SQL Database is the best option for create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations and uses the least amount of storage space, but our solution does not require a database management system (DBMS). Document database is best for unstructured data. Table storage is not suited for files.

[Explore databases - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-core-data-concepts/4-databases?ns-enrollment-type=learningpath&ns-enrollment-id=learn.wwl.azure-data-fundamentals-explore-core-data-concepts)

questions.name: 1.2_abc_DP900PA
questions.text: Which type of database should you use to store sequential data in the fastest way possible?
questions.textHtml: Which type of database should you use to store sequential data in the fastest way possible?
questions.rationale:
Time series databases are used to store sequential data. Table storage is not suited for time series. Graph databases are used to store hierarchical data, such as organizational charts that have nodes and edges. Azure SQL Database is the best option for create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations, uses the least amount of storage space, and is not optimized for time series.

[Understand data store models - Azure Application Architecture Guide | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/architecture/guide/technology-choices/data-store-overview)

[Explore databases - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-core-data-concepts/4-databases)
questions.rationaleHtml:

Graph databases are used to store hierarchical data, such as organizational charts that have nodes and edges. Azure SQL Database is the best option for create, read, update, and delete (CRUD) operations, uses the least amount of storage space, and is not optimized for time series.

**[Answer choice]** is a process to reduce duplicate data in a database and ensure data integrity.
questions.textHtml:
Select the answer that correctly completes the sentence.

[Answer choice] is a process to reduce duplicate data in a database and ensure data integrity.
questions.rationale:
Normalization is a process to reduce data duplication. This can be done by separating entities into their own tables and establishing relationships between the tables.

Creating an index allows you to search for data in a table more efficiently. A view is a virtual table based on the results of a SELECT query. It allows you to view specific rows in one or more tables. A stored procedure defines SQL statements that can be run on command.

questions.name: 2.1_baj_DP900PA
questions.text: Which three statements accurately describe the properties of a table in a relational database? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
questions.textHtml: Which three statements accurately describe the properties of a table in a relational database? Each correct answer presents a complete solution.
questions.rationale:
Each row in a table in a relational database has the same number of columns, can have a primary key built on multiple columns, and must have a data type. Tables in relational databases do not need all the columns to contain values or require that there be only one foreign key.

[Understand relational data - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-relational-data-offerings/2-understand-relational-data)
questions.rationaleHtml:
Each row in a table in a relational database has the same number of columns, can have a primary key built on multiple columns, and must have a data type. Tables in relational databases do not need all the columns to contain values or require that there be only one foreign key.

Understand relational data - Training | Microsoft Learn

questions.name: 2.1_bak_DP900PA
questions.text:
You need to recommend a solution that meets the following requirements:

- Encapsulates a business logic that can rename the products in a database
- Adds entries to tables

What should you include in the recommendation?
questions.textHtml:
You need to recommend a solution that meets the following requirements:

  • Encapsulates a business logic that can rename the products in a database
  • Adds entries to tables

What should you include in the recommendation?
questions.rationale:
A stored procedure can encapsulate any type of business logic that can be reused in the application. A stored procedure can modify existing data as well as add new entries to tables. A stored procedure can be run from an application as well as from the server.

An inline function cannot be used to complete the task because it cannot modify nor create objects. It can be used to query a database. A view cannot be used to complete the task because it cannot modify nor create objects. It can be used to query a database. A table-valued function cannot be used to complete the task because it cannot modify or create objects. It can be used to query a database.

[Describe database objects - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-relational-data-offerings/5-database-objects?ns-enrollment-type=learningpath&ns-enrollment-id=learn.wwl.azure-data-fundamentals-explore-relational-data)
questions.rationaleHtml:
A stored procedure can encapsulate any type of business logic that can be reused in the application. A stored procedure can modify existing data as well as add new entries to tables. A stored procedure can be run from an application as well as from the server.

An inline function cannot be used to complete the task because it cannot modify nor create objects. It can be used to query a database. A view cannot be used to complete the task because it cannot modify nor create objects. It can be used to query a database. A table-valued function cannot be used to complete the task because it cannot modify or create objects. It can be used to query a database.

Describe database objects - Training | Microsoft Learn

questions.name: 2.2_bba_DP900PA
questions.text: Which service is managed and serverless, avoids the use of Windows Server licenses, and allows for each workload to have its own instance of the service being used?
questions.textHtml: Which service is managed and serverless, avoids the use of Windows Server licenses, and allows for each workload to have its own instance of the service being used?
questions.rationale:
Azure SQL Database is a serverless platform as a service (PaaS) SQL instance. SQL Managed Instance is a PaaS service, but databases are maintained in the same SQL Managed Instance cluster. SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines running Windows or Linux are not serverless options.

[Identify data services - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-roles-responsibilities-world-of-data/3-data-services)
questions.rationaleHtml:
Azure SQL Database is a serverless platform as a service (PaaS) SQL instance. SQL Managed Instance is a PaaS service, but databases are maintained in the same SQL Managed Instance cluster. SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines running Windows or Linux are not serverless options.

questions.name: 2.2_bbk_DP900PA
questions.text: Which SQL engine is optimized for IoT scenarios?
questions.textHtml: Which SQL engine is optimized for IoT scenarios?
questions.rationale:
SQL Edge is optimized for IoT scenarios that must work with streaming time series data. SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machinesis is best used when you want to retain control over the server and database configuration. SQL Managed Instance is ideal for cloud migrations where you need minimal change to existing apps. Azure SQL Database is best used for new cloud solutions.

[Describe Azure SQL services and capabilities - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-provision-deploy-relational-database-offerings-azure/2-azure-sql)
questions.rationaleHtml:
SQL Edge is optimized for IoT scenarios that must work with streaming time series data. SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machinesis is best used when you want to retain control over the server and database configuration. SQL Managed Instance is ideal for cloud migrations where you need minimal change to existing apps. Azure SQL Database is best used for new cloud solutions.

Describe Azure SQL services and capabilities - Training | Microsoft Learn

questions.name: 4.1_dak_DP900PA
questions.text: Which type of Microsoft Fabric data store supports large-scale analytical workloads?
questions.textHtml: Which type of Microsoft Fabric data store supports large-scale analytical workloads?
questions.rationale:
**Objective:**

4.1 Describe common elements of large-scale analytics

**What This Item Tests:**

Describe options for analytical data stores

**Additional Reading:**

[Decision tree for analytical store selection in Microsoft Fabric - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/architecture/data-guide/technology-choices/fabric-analytical-data-stores#decision-tree-for-analytical-store-selection-in-microsoft-fabric)

**Rationale:**

Fabric provides multiple types of data stores that are optimized for different types of workloads. A data warehouse is specifically designed to support large-scale analytical workloads, offering optimized storage and query performance for complex, high-concurrency analytical queries over structured data. In contrast, a SQL database is intended for transactional or operational scenarios, a lakehouse combines data lake storage with analytical processing but is positioned as a flexible analytics option rather than the primary enterprise analytical store at the fundamentals level, and a eventhouse is optimized for real-time streaming and event data. Therefore, the data warehouse is the most appropriate analytical data store to support large-scale analytics.
questions.rationaleHtml:
Objective:

4.1 Describe common elements of large-scale analytics

What This Item Tests:

Describe options for analytical data stores

Additional Reading:

Decision tree for analytical store selection in Microsoft Fabric - Training | Microsoft Learn

Rationale:

Fabric provides multiple types of data stores that are optimized for different types of workloads. A data warehouse is specifically designed to support large-scale analytical workloads, offering optimized storage and query performance for complex, high-concurrency analytical queries over structured data. In contrast, a SQL database is intended for transactional or operational scenarios, a lakehouse combines data lake storage with analytical processing but is positioned as a flexible analytics option rather than the primary enterprise analytical store at the fundamentals level, and a eventhouse is optimized for real-time streaming and event data. Therefore, the data warehouse is the most appropriate analytical data store to support large-scale analytics.

questions.name: 4.2_dbj_DP900PA
questions.text:
You need to ingest, transform, and visualize data from a continuously generated data source. The solution must minimize latency and administrative effort.

Which type of data processing should you use?
questions.textHtml:
You need to ingest, transform, and visualize data from a continuously generated data source. The solution must minimize latency and administrative effort.

Which type of data processing should you use?
questions.rationale:
**Objective:**

4.2 Describe consideration for real-time data analytics

**What This Item Tests:**

Describe the difference between batch and streaming data

**Additional Reading:**

[Understand batch and stream processing - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/explore-fundamentals-stream-processing/2-batch-stream)  
  
[Stream processing systems | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/training/modules/cmu-message-queues-streams/3-stream-processing-systems)  
  
[Batch vs. streaming - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/databricks/data-engineering/batch-vs-streaming#batch-vs-streaming)  
  
[Streaming dataflows (preview) - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/transform-model/dataflows/dataflows-streaming)  
  
[Continuous data ingestion - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/data-explorer/ingest-data-overview#continuous-data-ingestion)  

**Rationale:**

Stream processing supports continuously generated data by processing events as they occur, which enables low-latency ingestion, transformation, and visualization while requiring less operational management than interval-based approaches. Batch processing introduces delays because data is collected and processed at scheduled times, and microbatching still processes data in small intervals rather than immediately. Although the term “real-time” is commonly used to describe low-latency scenarios, it is not a formal data processing model; stream processing is the appropriate classification for this type of workload.
questions.rationaleHtml:
Objective:

4.2 Describe consideration for real-time data analytics

What This Item Tests:

Describe the difference between batch and streaming data

Additional Reading:

Understand batch and stream processing - Training | Microsoft Learn

Stream processing systems | Microsoft Learn

Batch vs. streaming - Training | Microsoft Learn

Streaming dataflows (preview) - Training | Microsoft Learn

Continuous data ingestion - Training | Microsoft Learn

Rationale:

Stream processing supports continuously generated data by processing events as they occur, which enables low-latency ingestion, transformation, and visualization while requiring less operational management than interval-based approaches. Batch processing introduces delays because data is collected and processed at scheduled times, and microbatching still processes data in small intervals rather than immediately. Although the term “real-time” is commonly used to describe low-latency scenarios, it is not a formal data processing model; stream processing is the appropriate classification for this type of workload.

questions.name: 4.3_dcb_DP900PA
questions.text: Which type of visual in Microsoft Power BI should you use to compare categorized values as the proportions of a total value?
questions.textHtml: Which type of visual in Microsoft Power BI should you use to compare categorized values as the proportions of a total value?
questions.rationale:
Pie charts visually compare different values as a proportion of a total. Line charts are used to examine trends, usually over time. Bar charts are used to compare different values for discrete categories. Scatter plots are used to determine a relationship or correlation between two numeric values.

[Describe considerations for data visualization - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-fundamentals-data-visualization/4-data-visualizations)
questions.rationaleHtml:

questions.name: 3.1_cac_DP900PA
questions.text: Which type of Azure Storage is used to store large amounts of data that must be processed by services such as Azure Databricks, Azure Synapse Analytics, and Azure HDInsight?
questions.textHtml: Which type of Azure Storage is used to store large amounts of data that must be processed by services such as Azure Databricks, Azure Synapse Analytics, and Azure HDInsight?
questions.rationale:
Data Lake Storage Gen2 is used for storing huge amounts of data to be processed by services such as Databricks, Azure Synapse Analytics, and HDInsight. Page blobs are used for VHDs. Table storage is used for two dimensional tables. Azure Files is not optimized for large amounts of data to be processed by services such as HDInsight.

[Explore Azure DataLake Storage Gen2 - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-provision-deploy-non-relational-data-services-azure/3-azure-data-lake-gen2)
questions.rationaleHtml:
Data Lake Storage Gen2 is used for storing huge amounts of data to be processed by services such as Databricks, Azure Synapse Analytics, and HDInsight. Page blobs are used for VHDs. Table storage is used for two dimensional tables. Azure Files is not optimized for large amounts of data to be processed by services such as HDInsight.

Explore Azure DataLake Storage Gen2 - Training | Microsoft Learn

questions.textHtml: Which type of Azure Storage is used to store key/value pairs grouped in partitions?
questions.rationale:
Table storage is used to store key/value pairs in partitions. Azure Files is used to store and share files by using SMB and NFS. Data Lake Storage Gen2 is used to store structured and unstructured data for processing. Page blobs are used for VHDs.

[Explore Azure Tables - Training | Microsoft Learn](https://learn.microsoft.com/training/modules/explore-provision-deploy-non-relational-data-services-azure/5-azure-tables)
questions.rationaleHtml:

Explore Azure Tables - Training | Microsoft Learn

questions.rationale:
